title: Simus
content:

|death=5000 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]]<ref name="Date">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', which include the [[funeral of Marka Ragnos]] in [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]] and Simus' [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] in [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]], to [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]].</ref>
|species=[[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]]<ref name="Golden Age 3">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]]</ref>

{{Quote|The noblest among us…he always offered his wisdom.|Naga Sadow|<ref name="Golden Age 3" />}}
'''Simus''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] proficient in [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] who lived during the [[Golden Age of the Sith]]. He [[Sith training|trained]] the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Darth Naga Sadow]] and was one of the most powerful [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] of his [[Time/Legends|time]]. Over a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] before Sadow's ascension to the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire's]] [[Throne/Legends|throne]], Simus challenged fellow warlord [[Marka Ragnos]] for the mantle of Dark Lord. Although he was [[Sai cha/Legends|beheaded]] in a [[Duel between Marka Ragnos and Simus|duel]] against Ragnos around [[5100 BBY]], Simus clung onto his life—relying on a [[Unidentified Sith servant|Sith servant]] for mobility, the bodiless [[Sith sorcerer|magician]] preserved himself from [[Death/Legends|death]] by encasing his head in a [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] sphere.

Eventually, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Simus was made a part of Marka Ragnos' [[Sith Council]], becoming a respected member whose opinion was highly valued. As Ragnos pitted his adversaries against each other to ensure his hold on the throne, Simus [[Advisor/Legends|advised]] the Dark Lord, faithfully serving the Sith Empire. When Ragnos died in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] over a century later, Simus' [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], Naga Sadow, was one of two challengers for the mantle of Dark Lord. The other was [[Ludo Kressh]], a Sith Councilor who wished to keep the Empire hidden from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], which controlled much of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Sadow, however, wished to expand the Empire's borders. Although the Sith Council was split on the matter, Simus mediated between its clashing warlords whilst also providing support for his long-time friend Sadow, backing his candidacy for the Sith throne.

In Sadow's attempt to provide the other councilors with a reason to wage war against the Republic, Simus was [[First raid on the Sith Citadel|killed]] by his apprentice with a Republic-issue [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster]]. Sadow proceeded to frame the Republic for the respected magician's [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]], inflaming the Sith to initiate their [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|conquest]] of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Despite Sadow's efforts, the Golden Age of the Sith ended with the Republic's repelling and defeat of the Sith Empire. Just over a [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]] later, Simus was one of a few Sith whose names were used as part of a training program for [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith students]] in the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|empire]] of the Sith Lord [[Revan/Legends|Revan]].

==Biography==
===Duel with Marka Ragnos===
{{Quote|I remember when he lost his duel with Marka Ragnos…but he never gave up, never failed us…|Naga Sadow|<ref name="Golden Age 3" />}}
[[File:LudoVsSadow.jpg|thumb|left|272px|Ludo Kressh duels Naga Sadow for the Sith throne before Marka Ragnos' specter. Over a century earlier, Simus was beheaded in a duel against Ragnos for that throne.]]
[[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] several [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] before [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]],<ref name="Birth" /> Simus was a [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] who became one of the most powerful [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] of his [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> Beginning in [[5200 BBY]], Simus engaged in a power struggle with fellow [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Marka Ragnos]] to become the new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]].<ref name="Behind the Magic">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref> Around [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]]<ref name="CSWE Simus">{{CSWECite|III|152|Simus}} states that Simus challenged [[Marka Ragnos]] to a [[Duel between Marka Ragnos and Simus|duel]] more than 5100 [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which corresponds to [[5100 BBY/Legends|5100 BBY]]. Additionally, ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that Ragnos ruled the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] for a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] before his [[Death/Legends|death]], and ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' states that Ragnos's control of the Empire began after his victory in the duel. Therefore, the duel must have taken place in approximately 5100 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> Simus contended for the [[Throne/Legends|throne]] by challenged Ragnos to a [[duel between Marka Ragnos and Simus|duel]].<ref name="CSWE Simus" />

Simus was [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitated]] by Ragnos,<ref name="CSWE Simus" /> who assumed control of the Sith Empire.<ref name="Behind the Magic" /> However, as remembered over a century later by his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Simus was determined not to fail the Sith.<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> Using [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith techniques]] unknown to others, the [[Sith sorcerer|magician]] kept himself alive, preserving his head in a [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] sphere.<ref name="CSWE Simus" /> An [[Armor/Legends|armored]] [[Unidentified Sith servant|Sith]] served as the bearer of Simus' device, ferrying it as the beheaded master willed.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

===Golden Age of the Sith===
====Councilor of the Sith, Master of Naga Sadow====
{{Quote|Simus was my mentor, my teacher…|Naga Sadow speaks of his master|<ref name="Golden Age 3" />}}
Simus was eventually made a member of the [[Sith Council]], the Dark Lord's [[Advisor/Legends|advisory]] body composed of [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] that met at the [[Sith Citadel (Ziost)|Sith Citadel]] on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]],<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> located in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The bodiless warlord survived<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> the [[Assassin/Legends|assassinations]] and infighting brought about by Ragnos amongst his detractors,<ref name="DSS">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> instead advising the Dark Lord for over a hundred [[Standard year/Legends|years]]<ref name="CSWE Simus" /> and becoming greatly respected amongst fellow councilors.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

[[File:Simus_servant.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The decapitated Simus attended the Sith Council alongside his servant.]]
Aside from attending the Council, Simus took on a Sith named Naga Sadow as his apprentice.<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> Although most Sith during Marka Ragnos' reign were ignorant of their ancient enemies—the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and<ref name="DSS" /> the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] that governed much of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]—Sadow was [[Sith training|raised]] from birth to fight against purported threats of a Republic invasion and the replacement of Sith tenets with that of the rivalling [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]].<ref name="EG Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Amidst the frequent infighting amongst the Sith, which was stirred by Ragnos in his fear that further Sith expansion would result in contact with the Jedi and the Republic with disastrous effects,<ref name="DSS" /> Sadow became [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]] of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Khar Shian]] in Sith Space.<ref name="EG Warfare" />

When Ragnos [[Death/Legends|died]] around 5000 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> Simus' pupil bid for the position of Dark Lord against a fellow councilor named [[Ludo Kressh]]. Sadow was supported by his master as well as several other members of the Council in his aspiration to expand the borders of the Sith Empire,<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> while Kressh was a conservative contender with popular support, wanting to remain unchanged in their [[Golden Age of the Sith|golden age]]; keeping the Empire strong and stable before seeking new frontiers,<ref name="DSS" /> and to ensure that they retained their wealth and kept firm control over the Sith people.<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> Nevertheless, before a feud could be waged between the Sith, the late Marka Ragnos had to first be laid to rest at the ancient [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] on the [[Burial/Legends|burial]] world of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="Golden Age 2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]</ref>

====Procession on Korriban====
{{Quote|You speak with mindless fear, Ludo Kressh. Do not be so quick to destroy an opportunity.|Simus speaks to Ludo Kressh regarding the Daragon explorers|<ref name="Golden Age 3" />}}
Simus attended the [[funeral of Marka Ragnos]], where the burial ritual required all participating [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] to space themselves evenly in a perfect circle. The ceremony was conducted by twenty individuals, including Ludo Kressh, who led from the coveted sixth position, whereas Simus was in the sixteenth position located directly opposite.<ref name="TSL">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref> After the [[tomb of Marka Ragnos]] was sealed, Sadow challenged Kressh to a duel for the title of Dark Lord before the tomb.<ref name="Golden Age 2" /> While fighting, the combatants were interrupted by the appearance of a [[Force ghost/Legends|specter]] of Marka Ragnos, which told all the Sith present about the existence of their ancient foes, the Jedi, and that circumstances could soon change despite the current Golden Age of the Sith. Thus, before departing, the specter of Ragnos warned the Sith to choose their paths wisely.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

As the ghost of Simus' former rival dissipated, a foreign [[Starship/Legends|starship]] made landing on Korriban; after inputting random coordinates into the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] of their<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> [[Scout ship/Legends|scoutship]], the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', two [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] explorers from the Republic had inadvertently discovered the mausoleum world with a direct [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] between the Republic's [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and Sith Space. The off-worlders, [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Twin/Legends|twins]] named [[Jori Daragon|Jori]] and [[Gav Daragon]],<ref name="EG Warfare" /> proceeded to declare their intention of coming in peace and told the Sith that they hailed from the Republic. Upon hearing this, in their fear that the two Humans' arrival was the harbinger of a full-scale attack from the Republic Ragnos' specter had warned about,<ref name="Golden Age 2" /> the Sith Council immediately ordered the Daragons' [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] and convened to discuss the matter.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

===Dissension===
{{Quote|They ''[the Sith]'' must continually test their strength against each other… even if it means destroying themselves.|Kreia teaches Meetra Surik of the civil war between Naga Sadow and Ludo Kressh|<ref name="TSL" />}}
[[File:Simus1.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Simus realized that Sadow would advance his own plans at all costs.]]
Simus, who was accompanied by his servant, and nine other members of the Sith Council held a meeting on Ziost, a world from where the Dark Lord of the Sith normally ruled. As a new Dark Lord had yet to be decided on, the Council's warlords vied for rulership of the Empire, leaving Simus to mediate between the power-hungry Sith Lords. Ludo Kressh claimed that the two Humans were [[Spy/Legends|spies]] sent by the Republic who, if allowed to escape, would return with Republic forces to plunder all that the Sith had worked hard to build. Members of Kressh's [[Ludo Kressh's faction|conservative faction]] further said that they should kill the two [[Scout/Legends|explorers]] and remove all evidence that they were ever in Sith territory. Lord Simus then interjected, telling Kressh that he was overtaken by fear and constrained by irrational prudence. The dismembered Sith further told the council that the arrival of the Daragons was an opportunity—one they should consider before destroying it forever.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

Sadow sided with his master, telling Kressh that his mindset was still attached to the past times of Marka Ragnos and was afraid of change. Yet Kressh persisted, saying that if Simus was wrong, they would lose everything, while if he himself was wrong, they would lose but the lives of two worthless Humans. Sadow responded with anger, saying that to kill the two explorers was to lose the chance to pursue glory and riches, capture thousands of new worlds, and conquer the entire [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Holding Simus' gaze, Sadow declared that it was their destiny.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

===Death===
{{Quote|There will be no turning back… the other Sith Lords will draw their own conclusions.|Naga Sadow, after killing Simus|<ref name="Golden Age 3" />}}
Nevertheless, a majority of the Council members did not share Sadow and Simus' ambition and decided that Jori and Gav Daragon posed too great a threat to their Empire. The Council's<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> tribunal<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> sentenced the [[Prisoner/Legends|captives]] to immediate execution and resolved to discuss the foreign ship at the next meeting. Simus then traveled to the Daragons' prison cell within the cold depths of the Sith Citadel and, assisted by his servant, peered through an opening in the cell door and told the explorers of their fate as well as the Sith Council's rationale behind it. Gav Daragon protested that they had not even provided a testimony, but Simus dismissed him, replying that their information was irrelevant to the Council's deliberations.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

[[File:SimusDeath.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Naga Sadow shoots Simus.]]
Despite [[Politics/Legends|political]] opposition, Sadow was determined to see out his plan and secretly placed a [[Tracking device (Sith Empire)|tracking device]] on the Daragons' ship. Later, Simus discovered a [[First raid on the Sith Citadel|disruption]] in the cell block where the Daragons were being imprisoned on Ziost. Carried by his servant, the magician found Sadow, who stood facing away, alone in a corridor and surrounded by dozens of Sith corpses. Simus stated his concern for Sadow's wellbeing, believing that it had been a Republic raid. However, Sadow only turned towards his master and uttered the words "sorry, my old ally." As he spoke, Sadow shot Simus with a Republic-issue [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] he had taken from the ''Starbreaker 12'', shattering<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> the wizened Sith's crystal sphere and killing him instantly<ref name="CSWE Simus" /> with a single [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt also piercing through Simus' servant.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I might have considered the Republic a myth… until the terrible attack that killed Lord Simus.|Sith councilor Horak-mul falls for the manipulation of Naga Sadow|<ref name="Golden Age 4">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]]</ref>}}
Leaving the [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] as incriminating evidence against the Republic, Sadow left the scene of the murder. Simus' charred head was soon found by the Council, which mourned his death and was angered that someone would kill such a helpless opponent. The Sith declared the "Republic agents" monsters for having slain the veteran Council member, who was respected by even those who did not support his ideas. Sadow then burst in with the pretense of being oblivious to the goings on. Presented the remains of Simus by councilor [[Dor Gal-ram]], Sadow delivered a eulogy for his late mentor and successfully called for the Council to unite and ready to strike at the Republic, securing his own place as the new Dark Lord of the Sith. Left with [[Horak-mul]] and Dor Gal-ram as his only supporters on the Sith Council, Ludo Kressh stormed out of the Ziost citadel in defiance of what he saw as the impending destruction of everything he knew.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

[[File:Simus2.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Naga Sadow holds Simus' charred head.]]
Sadow saw Simus' death as a necessary sacrifice, as it sparked the same opportunities the late Sith had supported;<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> by manipulating the Sith into fear of impending Republic invasion, which resonated with many who were discontent with the lack of expansion of the Empire, Sadow was declared the successor to the Sith throne.<ref name="TSL" /> In the following [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]],<ref name="Fall 1">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]]</ref> the new Dark Lord of the Sith took Gav Daragon as his own apprentice, [[Battle of Khar Delba|defeated]] Kressh, and allowed Jori Daragon to escape back to the Republic with a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracker]] in place. Thus, the Sith Empire's forces were united and provided a pathway to Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]], allowing<ref name="NEC" /> [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Naga Sadow<ref name="EG Warfare" /> to launch the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], devastating the Republic with relentless, unpredictable tactics and his mastery of<ref name="NEC" /> old and forbidden<ref name="Fall 2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 2]]</ref> Sith magic.<ref name="NEC" />

The war left the Sith defeated and their empire shattered, however. Nevertheless, the Sith resurfaced to the wider galaxy multiple times.<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> The [[Mission to Dromund Kaas|rise]] of [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] and his new [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] in [[3960 BBY]]<ref name="TORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> revitalized a [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith training academy]] on Korriban, although Revan's empire fractured years later<ref name="JvS" /> and abandoned the site.<ref name="TSL" /> In [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="TSL date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' to [[3951 BBY]].</ref> a student [[Computer/Legends|terminal]] was found and operated within the [[Sith academy]] by the [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] Jedi [[Meetra Surik]] during her [[Teacher/Legends|tutelage]] under the fellow exile and former Sith Lord [[Kreia]]. The terminal's level one written test's second question described Marka Ragnos' funeral, testing [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith students']] skills in numerical problem-solving with the positioning of Sith Lords participating in the fallen Dark Lord's burial ritual. Aside from Ludo Kressh, Simus was the only member amongst the roster of Sith Lords who was named in the question.<ref name="TSL" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Naga Sadow, old friend—what has happened here? Are you hurt? ''[…]'' What… what are you doing?|Simus speaks to Naga Sadow upon reaching the latter's scene of slaughter|<ref name="Golden Age 3" />}}
[[File:Simus_prison.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Simus relied on a servant for mobility.]]
In his pursuit of ruling the Sith Empire, Simus was defeated and decapitated by Marka Ragnos in a duel. Simus persevered despite his loss, using his own proficiency in Sith magic to keep himself from death. While the Sith Lord was rendered immobile and had to rely on a servant, Simus faithfully served the Sith Empire as part of the Sith Council throughout and beyond Ragnos' reign.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

Simus was respected by other Sith Council members across political divisions and, while other councilors remained skeptical of Naga Sadow, had faith in his apprentice. The august magician referred to Sadow as "old friend" and shared the aspirant Dark Lord's ambition to expand the Sith Empire's territories and conquer the galaxy. The alleged Republic murder of the wizened and frail councilor drew emotional reactions amongst his colleagues, inflaming wider support for attacking the Republic and leading most of the Sith Empire's warlords to pledge their domains to Sadow as their new Dark Lord. In his eulogy, Sadow stated that Simus was the most noble amongst the Sith Councilors, always providing his wisdom.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

Along with wrinkled facial features, the beheaded Simus sported short [[Color/Legends|dark]] hair, a pair of red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and pale, gray-colored skin.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|They've killed old Simus!''"<br />"''They are monsters.|Dor Gal-ram and another [[Unidentified Sith Councilor (crystal vessel)|Sith Councilor]] present Simus' remains to Naga Sadow|<ref name="Golden Age 3" />}}
Simus challenged Marka Ragnos,<ref name="CSWE Simus" /> a Sith powerful both physically and in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]],<ref name="TSL" /> to battle for the position of Dark Lord of the Sith. Although the brawl ended with Simus' decapitation at the hands of Marka Ragnos, the defeated warlord made use of his knowledge of Sith magic to remain alive, encasing his own head in a crystal sphere and serving as an advisor to Ragnos.<ref name="CSWE Simus" /> Darth Naga Sadow, who led the Sith Empire in waging a devastating war against the Galactic Republic<ref name="NEC" /> with his political cunning,<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> leadership, and use of old and forbidden Sith magic,<ref name="Fall 2" /> was trained by Lord Simus.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

==Equipment==
The decapitated Simus made use of a crystal sphere encasing his severed head, which was perched atop a wider base. The [[Life support/Legends|life-support]] container was structured by opaque bars fitted between the base and a concave covering at the top, with transparent orange-colored material in between the bars<ref name="CSWE Simus" /> through which the inset head spoke and saw. Laid within the crystalline container, the Sith Lord relied on a servant for transportation.<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SimusPromo.jpg|thumb|right|224px|A preliminary sketch of Simus]]
Created for the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series' [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith|''Golden Age of the Sith'']] sub-series, Simus first appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|third issue]] of the sub-series.<ref name="Golden Age 3" /> Published on [[December 11]], [[1996]]<ref name="Marvel3">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54592/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996_3|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Golden Age of the Sith (1996) #3}}</ref> by [[Dark Horse Comics]], the comic issue was authored by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="Golden Age 3" />

Simus was first depicted in a preliminary sketch done by Dario Carrasco, Jr.<ref name="Golden Age 0">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]]</ref> that was published in [[July 31]], 1996,<ref name="Marvel0">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54589/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Golden Age of the Sith (1996)}}</ref> in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|prologue issue]] of the ''Golden Age of the Sith'' series. Unlike the character's finalized appearance in ''The Fabric of an Empire'', Simus is depicted without a container around his head.<ref name="Golden Age 0" />
